title: Learning to Ride a Bicycle
summary: A teenage boy describes how he learned to ride a bicycle with help from his father. He talks about his struggles, his falls, and eventually his success in riding on his own.
body: I was eleven years old when I finally learned to ride a bicycle. Most of my friends already knew how, but I was afraid to try because I did not want to fall. My dad decided to help me one Saturday morning in the park near our house. First he held the back of the seat so the bike would not tip over. I tried to pedal but I kept losing my balance. I fell three times in the first hour and my knees got quite scratched. I felt embarrassed but my dad told me not to give up. He said everyone falls when they first learn. After a short break we tried again. This time I focused more on looking ahead instead of looking down at my feet. That made a big difference. My dad slowly let go of the seat and I did not notice at first. When I realised he was not holding the bike anymore, I got nervous and wobbled, but I kept going. By the end of the morning I could ride about fifty metres without any help. I was so proud of myself. Now I ride my bike to school every day and I cannot imagine not knowing how to do it.

annotations.exampleEn: The glass tipped over and spilled water on the table.
She pedalled hard to get up the steep hill.
She fell off her scooter and got her elbows scratched.
He felt embarrassed when he got the answer wrong in front of the class.
Don't give up — you're almost there!
She let go of the rope and swam to the edge of the pool.
The new cyclist wobbled on the first turn but stayed on the bike.
I was so proud of myself when I finished the race.
I cannot imagine living without music.

grammarNotes.title: Past continuous for background action: 'I kept losing my balance'
grammarNotes.bodyVi: 'Keep + V-ing' diễn tả một hành động lặp đi lặp lại, thường là hành động gây phiền hoặc ngoài ý muốn. 'I kept losing my balance.' — Tôi cứ mãi mất thăng bằng. 'I kept looking down.' — Tôi cứ nhìn xuống. Cấu trúc này khác với 'I lost my balance' (một lần) hay 'I was losing my balance' (đang diễn ra tại một thời điểm). Ngoài ra, 'was + V-ing' được dùng để mô tả hành động đang diễn ra trong quá khứ khi một hành động khác xảy ra.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: The structure 'keep + -ing verb' describes a repeated or continuous action, often frustrating or unintended. 'I kept losing my balance' means it happened again and again. Compare: 'I lost my balance' (once) vs 'I kept losing my balance' (repeatedly). Also in this passage, the past continuous ('was holding', 'was not holding') describes an ongoing action at a specific past moment: 'My dad slowly let go of the seat and I did not notice at first.'

grammarNotes.title: 'Instead of + -ing' to contrast two actions
grammarNotes.bodyVi: 'Instead of + V-ing' dùng để so sánh hai hành động — hành động được chọn và hành động bị thay thế. 'I focused on looking ahead instead of looking down at my feet.' — Tôi tập trung nhìn về phía trước thay vì nhìn xuống chân. Cấu trúc này rất hữu ích khi giải thích lý do tại sao một lựa chọn tốt hơn lựa chọn kia. Sau 'instead of' luôn dùng V-ing (gerund).
grammarNotes.bodyEn: The structure 'instead of + gerund (-ing form)' contrasts two actions, showing that one was chosen over the other. 'I focused on looking ahead instead of looking down at my feet.' This was the key change that helped him balance. After 'instead of', always use the -ing form of the verb, not the infinitive. Other examples: 'He walked to school instead of taking the bus.' / 'Try listening instead of just reading.'

translationVi: Tôi mười một tuổi khi cuối cùng học được cách đi xe đạp. Hầu hết bạn bè tôi đã biết đi, nhưng tôi sợ thử vì không muốn bị ngã. Bố tôi quyết định giúp tôi vào một sáng thứ bảy trong công viên gần nhà. Đầu tiên anh giữ phía sau yên xe để xe không bị ngã. Tôi cố đạp nhưng cứ mãi mất thăng bằng. Tôi ngã ba lần trong giờ đầu tiên và đầu gối bị trầy xước khá nhiều. Tôi cảm thấy xấu hổ nhưng bố nói tôi đừng bỏ cuộc. Anh nói ai cũng ngã khi mới học. Sau một lúc nghỉ ngắn chúng tôi thử lại. Lần này tôi tập trung nhìn về phía trước thay vì nhìn xuống chân. Điều đó tạo ra sự khác biệt lớn. Bố từ từ buông tay ra khỏi yên xe và lúc đầu tôi không chú ý. Khi nhận ra anh không còn giữ xe nữa, tôi trở nên lo lắng và lắc lư, nhưng tôi tiếp tục đi. Vào cuối buổi sáng tôi có thể đi được khoảng năm mươi mét mà không cần giúp đỡ. Tôi tự hào về bản thân lắm. Bây giờ tôi đi xe đạp đến trường mỗi ngày và không thể tưởng tượng mình không biết làm điều đó.

questions.prompt: How old was the narrator when he learned to ride a bicycle?
questions.explanation: 'I was eleven years old when I finally learned to ride a bicycle.'
questions.hint: His exact age is given in the first sentence.

questions.prompt: Why was he afraid to try riding a bicycle?
options: He did not want to fall
He thought it was too difficult
He did not have a bicycle
questions.explanation: 'I was afraid to try because I did not want to fall.'
questions.hint: He gives a specific reason using 'because'.

options: On the street outside their house
In the park near their house
questions.explanation: 'My dad decided to help me one Saturday morning in the park near our house.'
questions.hint: The location is described in relation to where they live.

questions.prompt: What did his dad do to help him at first?
options: He held the back of the seat
questions.explanation: 'First he held the back of the seat so the bike would not tip over.'
questions.hint: He held a specific part of the bike.

questions.prompt: How many times did he fall in the first hour?
questions.explanation: 'I fell three times in the first hour.'

questions.hint: The word used is a mild form of injury.

questions.prompt: What did his dad say to encourage him?
options: He said he was doing great
He said everyone falls when they first learn
He said the bike was too big for him
questions.explanation: 'He said everyone falls when they first learn.'
questions.hint: His dad's advice was meant to normalise the experience.

questions.prompt: What important change did the narrator make the second time?
options: He focused on looking ahead instead of down
questions.explanation: 'I focused more on looking ahead instead of looking down at my feet.'
questions.hint: He changed where he directed his eyes.

questions.prompt: What did his dad do without him noticing?
options: He stepped back to take a photo
He slowly let go of the seat
He called to him from the distance
questions.explanation: 'My dad slowly let go of the seat and I did not notice at first.'
questions.hint: His dad made a quiet, gradual move.

questions.prompt: How did the narrator react when he realised his dad had let go?
options: He called out to his dad
He got nervous and wobbled but kept going
questions.explanation: 'I got nervous and wobbled, but I kept going.'
questions.hint: He had a reaction but pushed through it.

questions.prompt: How far could he ride without help by the end of the morning?
questions.explanation: 'I could ride about fifty metres without any help.'

questions.prompt: How does he use his bike now?
options: He rides it on weekends only
He rides it to school every day
He uses it for exercise in the park
questions.explanation: 'Now I ride my bike to school every day.'
questions.hint: He mentions a regular everyday use.

cloze.template: I was eleven years old when I finally learned to ride a {{b1}}. Most of my friends already knew how, but I was {{b2}} to try because I did not want to fall. My dad decided to help me one Saturday morning in the {{b3}} near our house. First he held the back of the {{b4}} so the bike would not tip over. I tried to pedal but I kept losing my {{b5}}. I fell three times in the first hour and my knees got quite {{b6}}. I felt embarrassed but my dad told me not to {{b7}}. He said everyone falls when they first learn. This time I focused on looking {{b8}} instead of looking down at my feet. That made a big {{b9}}. My dad slowly {{b10}} go of the seat and I did not notice at first. When I realised he was not holding the bike, I got nervous and {{b11}}, but I kept going. By the end of the morning I could ride about fifty metres without any {{b12}}.

blanks.explanation: 'bicycle' — the story is about learning to ride a bicycle.
'afraid' — he was afraid to try because he did not want to fall.
'park' — they practised in the park near their house.
'seat' — his dad held the back of the seat to keep the bike stable.
'balance' — he kept losing his balance, making the bike wobble.
'scratched' — his knees got scratched from the falls.
'give up' — his dad encouraged him not to give up.
'ahead' — looking ahead (in front) rather than down was the key change.
'difference' — 'made a big difference' means it had a significant positive effect.
'let' — 'let go of' is the fixed phrase meaning to release something.
'wobbled' — he wobbled (moved unsteadily) but kept going.
'help' — riding without help means doing it independently.

criticalThinkingQuestion: The narrator says his dad told him 'everyone falls when they first learn.' Learning from failure is an important part of developing any skill. Can you think of something you found very difficult to learn? What helped you to keep trying and not give up?
